title: Operation First Contact
learning_objectives: Execute a complete external assessment simulation
Combine scanning, enumeration, and vulnerability mapping systematically
Produce a professional client-ready assessment report
scenario: Following the passive recon phase, the financial services company has authorised active scanning and enumeration of their external-facing lab environment (3 servers). Your objective: discover everything about their attack surface through authorised scanning. Enumerate all services, map vulnerabilities, and produce a professional assessment report.

scenario_context.setting: Financial services company's external-facing lab environment (3 servers)
scenario_context.challenge: Complete external assessment from pre-engagement to final report
scenario_context.relevance: External assessments are the most common engagement type for junior pentesters. This capstone simulates the full workflow.

subtasks.title: Pre-Engagement Setup
subtasks.description: Complete authorisation documentation, define scope, configure scanning tools, and verify lab connectivity.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Reuse skills from OB-M17. Complete scope document. Verify all tools. Confirm connectivity to all 3 targets. Document everything before starting.
subtasks.evidence_required: Authorisation docs, scope, and connectivity proof

subtasks.title: Host Discovery & Port Scanning
subtasks.description: Discover all live hosts and perform comprehensive port scanning using multiple techniques.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Apply OB-M15 skills at scale. Host discovery. Multiple scan types. Compare results. Document all findings per host.
subtasks.evidence_required: Host discovery and port scan results with analysis

subtasks.title: Service Enumeration & Fingerprinting
subtasks.description: Enumerate all discovered services: versions, configurations, default credentials, directory structures.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Apply OB-M14 skills. Full service enumeration per host. Web directory enumeration. SMB/FTP enumeration where applicable. Document everything.
subtasks.evidence_required: Complete service enumeration report per host

subtasks.title: Vulnerability Mapping
subtasks.description: Cross-reference all discovered services with CVE databases. Assess exploitability and prioritise.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Apply OB-M13 skills. For each service version: search NVD, check ExploitDB. Assess exploitability. Prioritise by risk considering client context.

subtasks.title: Professional Assessment Report
subtasks.description: Compile a client-ready assessment report with executive summary, methodology, findings, risk ratings, and remediation roadmap.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Structure: Cover page, Executive Summary, Scope & Methodology, Host-by-Host Findings, Vulnerability Summary, Risk Ratings, Remediation Roadmap, Appendices. Client-ready quality.

reflection_prompt: You complete the assessment and find that one server has a critical vulnerability that is actively being exploited in the wild. The client's remediation timeline is 30 days. What do you recommend for the interim?

quiz.question: A professional assessment report should always begin with:
options: An executive summary that communicates risk in business terms
quiz.explanation: The executive summary is the most-read section. It must communicate risk clearly for non-technical decision-makers.

quiz.question: A remediation roadmap is valuable because:
options: It prioritises fixes by risk and effort, giving the client a clear action plan
quiz.explanation: A roadmap helps clients allocate limited resources effectively, addressing the highest risks first with clear timelines.

portfolio_artifacts: Complete assessment report with remediation roadmap
mentor_notes: This is the culminating assessment. Evaluate as a professional deliverable that would be sent to a real client. The report quality is the primary scoring factor. Scope compliance must be perfect.
